lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਜਾਚਿਕੁ ਤੂ ਅਲਖ ਅਭੇਉ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: hau jaachik too alakh abheo |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I am just a beggar; You are invisible and unknowable. ||1||Pause||
Such is the Name of the Immaculate, Divine Lord.
Love of Maya is like a cursed woman,
But when one is blessed with the Naam, the darkness within is illuminated. ||2||
Ugly, dirty and promiscuous.
Power and beauty are false, and last for only a few days.
I tasted Maya and renounced it, and now, I have no doubts.
One whose father is known, cannot be illegitimate.
One who belongs to the One Lord, has no fear.
The Creator acts, and causes all to act. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਸੂਝੈ ਗੁਰ ਕਉ ਵਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: avar na soojhai gur kau vaariaa |
lines.english: He does not know any other, and he is a sacrifice to the Guru.
Keeping his mind restrained, he enshrines the True Lord within his heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਦਿ ਮੁਏ ਮਨੁ ਮਨ ਤੇ ਮਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: sabad mue man man te maariaa |
lines.english: One who dies in the Word of the Shabad conquers his mind, through his mind.
O Nanak, attuned to the Naam, he is emancipated. ||4||3||
Through the Word of the Guru's Teachings, the mind intuitively meditates on the Lord.
Bilaaval, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਕਿਉ ਰਹੀਐ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦਿ ਪਛਾਨੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: har bin kiau raheeai gur sabad pachhaane |1|
lines.english: Without the Lord, how can anyone survive? Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, He is realized. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੈ ਰੰਗਿ ਰਤਾ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਨੇ ॥
lines.translit: har kai rang rataa man maane |
lines.english: Imbued with the Lord's Love, the mind is satisfied.
The insane, self-willed manmukhs wander around, deluded by doubt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਜੀਅਰਾ ਰਹਿ ਨ ਸਕੈ ਖਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਬੂਝ ਬੁਝਾਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har bin jeearaa reh na sakai khin satigur boojh bujhaaee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Without the Lord, my soul cannot survive, even for an instant; the True Guru has helped me understand this. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਦਰਸਨ ਕੈਸੇ ਜੀਵਉ ਮੇਰੀ ਮਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: bin darasan kaise jeevau meree maaee |
lines.english: Without the Blessed Vision of His Darshan, how can I live, O my mother?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਸਿ ਗਿਰਾਸਿ ਜਪਉ ਅਪੁਨੇ ਹਰਿ ਭਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: saas giraas jpau apune har bhaalee |
lines.english: With each breath and morsel of food, I meditate on my Lord, and seek Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰਾ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਬਿਸਰੈ ਹਉ ਮਰਉ ਦੁਖਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: meraa prabh bisarai hau mrau dukhaalee |
lines.english: Forgetting my God, I die in pain.
The Unspoken Speech is spoken, by the Will of the Guru.
Now, as Gurmukh, I know that the Lord is always with me. ||2||
I remain always detached, but I am enraptured with the Lord's Name.
He shows us that God is unapproachable and unfathomable.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਮੇਟਿ ਚਲੈ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦਿ ਸਮਾਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: haumai mett chalai gur sabad samaae |3|
lines.english: Eradicating egotism, and walking in harmony with the Guru's Will, I am absorbed in the Word of the Shabad. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਕਰਣੀ ਕਿਆ ਕਾਰ ਕਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin gur karanee kiaa kaar kamaae |
lines.english: Without the Guru, what lifestyle could we practice, and what work could we do?
The Gurmukhs are celebrated with the glory of the Naam, the Name of the Lord.
The self-willed manmukhs are separated from the Lord, gathering false wealth.
The Lord has showered His Mercy upon me, and made me the slave of His slaves.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮ ਧਨੁ ਰਾਸਿ ॥੪॥੪॥
lines.translit: jan naanak har naam dhan raas |4|4|
lines.english: The Name of the Lord is the wealth and capital of servant Nanak. ||4||4||
Bilaaval, Third Mehl, First House:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਖਾਇਆ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਸੋਇਆ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਕਾਪੜੁ ਅੰਗਿ ਚੜਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: dhrig dhrig khaaeaa dhrig dhrig soeaa dhrig dhrig kaaparr ang charraaeaa |
lines.english: Cursed, cursed is the food; cursed, cursed is the sleep; cursed, cursed are the clothes worn on the body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਸਰੀਰੁ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਸਹਿਤ ਸਿਉ ਜਿਤੁ ਹੁਣਿ ਖਸਮੁ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: dhrig sareer kuttanb sahit siau jit hun khasam na paaeaa |
lines.english: Cursed is the body, along with family and friends, when one does not find his Lord and Master in this life.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ਛੁੜਕੀ ਫਿਰਿ ਹਾਥਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਅਹਿਲਾ ਜਨਮੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: paurree chhurrakee fir haath na aavai ahilaa janam gavaaeaa |1|
lines.english: He misses the step of the ladder, and this opportunity will not come into his hands again; his life is wasted, uselessly. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਗਜੀਵਨ ਦਾਤਾ ਜਨ ਸੇਵਕ ਤੇਰੇ ਤਿਨ ਕੇ ਤੈ ਦੂਖ ਨਿਵਾਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jagajeevan daataa jan sevak tere tin ke tai dookh nivaare |1| rahaau |
lines.english: O Life of the World, O Great Giver, you eradicate the sorrows of your humble servants. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਜਾ ਭਾਉ ਨ ਦੇਈ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗਣਿ ਜਿਨਿ ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਚਰਣ ਵਿਸਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: doojaa bhaau na deee liv laagan jin har ke charan visaare |
lines.english: The love of duality does not allow him to lovingly focus his attention on the Lord; he forgets the Feet of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਦਇਆਪਤਿ ਦਾਤਾ ਕਿਆ ਏਹਿ ਜੰਤ ਵਿਚਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: too deaal deaapat daataa kiaa ehi jant vichaare |
lines.english: You are Merciful, O Great Giver of Mercy; what are these poor beings?

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਕਤ ਬੰਧ ਸਭਿ ਤੁਝ ਤੇ ਹੋਏ ਐਸਾ ਆਖਿ ਵਖਾਣੇ ॥
lines.translit: mukat bandh sabh tujh te hoe aisaa aakh vakhaane |
lines.english: All are liberated or placed into bondage by You; this is all one can say.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਸੋ ਮੁਕਤੁ ਕਹੀਐ ਮਨਮੁਖ ਬੰਧ ਵਿਚਾਰੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: guramukh hovai so mukat kaheeai manamukh bandh vichaare |2|
lines.english: One who becomes Gurmukh is said to be liberated, while the poor self-willed manmukhs are in bondage. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਕੀ ਗਹਣ ਗਤਿ ਕਹੀ ਨ ਜਾਈ ਸਚੈ ਆਪਿ ਸਵਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: tin kee gehan gat kahee na jaaee sachai aap savaare |
lines.english: His depth and condition cannot be described. The True Lord Himself embellishes him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਜਨੁ ਮੁਕਤੁ ਜਿਸੁ ਏਕ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗੀ ਸਦਾ ਰਹੈ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: so jan mukat jis ek liv laagee sadaa rahai har naale |
lines.english: He alone is liberated, who lovingly focuses his attention on the One Lord, always dwelling with the Lord.
